fields.summary: REST API to trigger storing auxiliary manifest file and publish to NMs
[Umbrella] Dynamically add or remove auxiliary services

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: System administrator can change auxiliary service by configuration and restart node managers for the change to take effect.  The new auxiliary service design allows service manifest file to be stored in hdfs or local file system.  This task is to create a set of REST API to update auxiliary service manifest file, and communication protocol to notify NMs to reload auxiliary services.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: Patch 01 is a first step towards improving the mechanism for reloading auxiliary services. It adds an NM REST API for updating the auxiliary services on that NM. It does not modify the manifest file in HDFS, so the admin would need to do that manually if desired. This REST API would allow the aux services manifest polling to be turned off, while still giving the admin a method of updating the aux services on a running NM without restarting the NM (but a REST call would need to be performed for each NM).

comments.body: Patch 01 is a first step towards improving the mechanism for reloading auxiliary services. It adds an NM REST API for updating the auxiliary services on that NM. It does not modify the manifest file in HDFS, so the admin would need to do that manually if desired. This REST API would allow the aux services manifest polling to be turned off, while still giving the admin a method of updating the aux services on a running NM without restarting the NM (but a REST call would need to be performed for each NM).
